pattern: With no access to the head, a node can only erase itself by impersonation: copy the next node's value into itself, then splice that now-redundant neighbor out.
hints: You cannot walk backward from this node to unlink it the normal way — there is no head reference available.
Instead of removing this node, overwrite its value with the following node's value.
Then point this node's next past the following node, discarding it; the list now reads as if this node were gone.

edge_inputs.description: delete a different middle node in the same list
delete the head of a three-node list
two-node list, delete the (non-tail) first node
value 0 present and being deleted — guards against falsy-value bugs
delete the second-to-last node so the new tail's next must become null
longer list, delete near the end

stress.description: random unique-valued list, deleting a random non-tail node
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 500))
    vals = rng.sample(range(-1000, 1001), n)
    idx = rng.randint(0, n - 2)
    return [vals, vals[idx]]
